title: Attention Head Scaling
concept: Increasing the number of attention heads (callback to Unit 267) allows parallel extraction of diverse contextual relationships.

exercises.prompt: From what you remember about attention in AI, what do 'attention heads' do?

options.text: They help the model focus on relationships between different words.
They are physical screens attached to the computer.
They are used to count the number of letters in a word.

exercises.explain: Attention heads are the components in transformer models that calculate how words relate to each other in a sentence.

cards.title: Too Many Tasks, Too Few Eyes
cards.body: Have you ever tried to read a book while looking for spelling mistakes, translating the story in your head, and trying to remember who the killer is all at once? It's exhausting! AI models face the same struggle when trying to understand language. To solve this, they use 'multiple heads' to share the workload.

cards.title: What is Head Scaling?
cards.body: Attention Head Scaling means increasing the number of attention heads in a transformer model. Instead of having just one attention mechanism look at a sentence, we run multiple attention heads in parallel. This allows the AI to capture different kinds of relationships between words at the exact same time.

cards.title: The Detective Squad
cards.body: Imagine a detective solving a complex mystery. If there is only one detective (one attention head), they can only focus on one clue at a time, like looking for fingerprints. If you hire a team of ten detectives (ten attention heads), they can work at the same time: one looks for fingerprints, another tracks footprints, a third interviews witnesses, and a fourth checks the clock. They all study the same crime scene but find different clues simultaneously!

cards.title: Multi-Tasking in Action
cards.body:
In the sentence: 'The chef cooked the soup because it was cold.'

• Head 1 might connect 'it' to 'soup' (resolving pronouns).
• Head 2 might connect 'cooked' to 'chef' (connecting actions to people).
• Head 3 might connect 'soup' to 'cold' (linking objects to descriptions).

By scaling the heads, the AI understands all these connections at once!

cards.title: Myth: More Heads = Longer Text
cards.body: Many people think that adding more attention heads makes the AI look at longer sentences. That's a myth! More heads don't increase the text limit; they just allow the AI to analyze the same sentence in more detail, looking for multiple patterns at once.

cards.title: One Head vs. Many Heads
cards.body: Compare how a model processes a sentence with only one head versus multiple scaled heads.

left.text: Focuses on only one relationship at a time (e.g., just matching verbs to nouns).

right.text: Focuses on pronouns, descriptions, and grammar patterns all at the same time.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of having multiple attention heads in an AI model?

options.text: To make the model run much slower on purpose
To look for different types of word relationships at the same time
To translate the text into different human languages instantly
To delete words that are not important to the sentence

exercises.explain: Attention heads allow the model to focus on different aspects of the text (like grammar, pronouns, or action-object links) simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Increasing the number of attention heads helps the AI understand complex sentence structures better.
exercises.explain: More attention heads allow the model to capture more diverse relationships in the text, improving its understanding.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how scaled attention heads work.
exercises.sentence: Having multiple attention heads allows the model to process different word connections in ________.
exercises.explain: By processing in parallel, different attention heads can look for different relationships in the text at the exact same time.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes scaled attention heads working correctly?

options.text: Head A links nouns to verbs, while Head B links pronouns to nouns at the same time.
One single head slowly checks the sentence five times in a row for different rules.
The AI system completely shuts down to save energy when a long sentence arrives.

exercises.explain: Option B shows a single head trying to do everything sequentially, whereas Option A correctly shows scaled heads working on different relationships at the same time. Option C is unrelated to attention heads.

exercises.prompt: Why does scaling up (increasing) the number of attention heads improve an AI's understanding of text?

options.text: Each head is forced to read a much longer sentence than before
Each head can look for a different type of word relationship in parallel
All heads are forced to look at the exact same word connection
The model loses the ability to process words at the same time

exercises.explain: Adding more attention heads doesn't make each head work harder or look at more words at once; instead, it allows them to split the work and focus on completely different types of relationships (like verbs, grammar, or pronouns) at the same time.
